chapters.title: Chemical Reactions and Equations
topics: Types of reactions: Combination, Decomposition, Displacement, Double Displacement, Redox
Effects of oxidation in daily life — Corrosion and Rancidity
equations: CuO + H₂ → Cu + H₂O (Redox: CuO reduced, H₂ oxidized)
importantQuestions: What is a balanced chemical equation? Why should it be balanced?
Explain different types of chemical reactions with examples.
What is corrosion? How can you prevent it?
Distinguish between combination and decomposition reactions with examples.
tips: Balancing mein Hit and Trial method use karo — sabse aasan hai
Reaction types identify karne ke liye: products mein kitne compounds hain dekho
Decomposition = 1 reactant → many products; Combination = many → 1
Symbols yaad rakho: ↑(gas), ↓(precipitate), Δ(heat), ⇌(reversible)

chapters.title: Acids, Bases and Salts
topics: Chemical properties of acids and bases
Reaction of acids with metals, metal carbonates, metal bicarbonates
pH scale and importance in daily life
Salts: Family, pH, Chemicals from salts (NaOH, Bleaching powder, Baking soda, Washing soda, Plaster of Paris)
equations: NaCl + NH₃ + CO₂ + H₂O → NaHCO₃ + NH₄Cl
2NaHCO₃ → Na₂CO₃ + H₂O + CO₂ (Washing soda from baking soda)
importantQuestions: What happens when an acid solution is mixed with a base solution? (Write word and chemical equation)
How is Plaster of Paris prepared? What happens when it is mixed with water?
Explain the Chlor-alkali process with diagram.
Why does tooth decay start when pH of mouth falls below 5.5?
tips: pH scale 0-14: 0-7 acidic, 7 neutral, 7-14 basic — yeh fundamental hai
Bleaching powder, Baking soda, Washing soda — teen chemicals ke formulas aur uses ratto
Plaster of Paris: CaSO₄·½H₂O — half water of crystallisation yaad rakho
Neutralisation = Acid + Base → Salt + Water — har jagah applicable

chapters.title: Metals and Non-metals
topics: Physical properties of metals and non-metals
Ionic bonding — formation of ionic compounds
Occurrence of metals — minerals and ores
Extraction of metals — Enrichment, Reduction, Refining
equations: ZnO + C → Zn + CO (Reduction by carbon)
importantQuestions: Write the reactivity series of metals. Why is gold found in free state in nature?
Explain the extraction of metals of medium reactivity from their oxides.
What is thermite reaction? Where is it used?
Explain the formation of NaCl with electron transfer diagram.
Differentiate between roasting and calcination with examples.
tips: Reactivity series ratta lagao: K Na Ca Mg Al Zn Fe Ni Sn Pb H Cu Hg Ag Au Pt
Extraction method reactivity pe depend karta hai: High→Electrolysis, Medium→Carbon reduction, Low→Self-reduction
Ionic bond mein electron transfer hota hai — diagram banao Na → Na⁺ + e⁻ and Cl + e⁻ → Cl⁻
Roasting = sulphide ore + oxygen; Calcination = carbonate ore + heat (bina air)

chapters.title: Carbon and its Compounds
topics: Versatile nature of carbon — Catenation, Tetravalency
Chemical properties of carbon compounds: Combustion, Oxidation, Addition, Substitution
Ethanol and Ethanoic acid — properties and uses
equations: CH₃CH₂OH + O₂ → CH₃COOH + H₂O (Ethanol → Ethanoic acid - Oxidation)
CH₄ + Cl₂ → CH₃Cl + HCl (Substitution, in sunlight)
importantQuestions: What is homologous series? Write properties of homologous series.
What are the two properties of carbon that lead to huge number of carbon compounds?
Explain the cleansing action of soaps with diagram.
What is the difference between soap and detergent?
How would you distinguish saturated from unsaturated hydrocarbons?
tips: IUPAC naming: Root word (C count) + suffix (-ane, -ene, -yne) + functional group
Meth=1, Eth=2, Prop=3, But=4, Pent=5 carbon — yeh yaad karo
Saponification = Fat + Base → Soap + Glycerol — diagram ke saath samjho
Ethanol properties: dehydration at 443K (with conc. H₂SO₄), reaction with Na
Micelle formation ka diagram important hai for soap cleaning action

chapters.title: Periodic Classification of Elements
topics: Modern Periodic Table — based on atomic number
Position of elements — groups and periods
Trends in properties: Valency, Atomic size, Metallic/Non-metallic character, Electronegativity
importantQuestions: State the modern periodic law. How does it differ from Mendeleev's periodic law?
How do atomic size and metallic character vary in a group and period?
Why are noble gases placed in a separate group?
List any 3 limitations of Mendeleev's periodic table.
An element has atomic number 12. What is its group, period, and valency?
tips: Group mein neeche jaao → Atomic size badhta hai, Metallic character badhta hai
Period mein left to right → Atomic size ghatta hai, Non-metallic character badhta hai
Atomic number = number of protons = number of electrons (for neutral atom)
Mendeleev ne mass se arrange kiya, Modern table atomic number se — yeh key difference hai

chapters.title: Life Processes
topics: Human digestive system — organs and enzymes
Transportation in humans: Heart, Blood vessels, Blood
Transportation in plants: Xylem and Phloem
Excretion in humans: Nephron, Kidney function
importantQuestions: Draw a diagram of human digestive system and label: Liver, Pancreas, Small intestine, Large intestine
Describe the process of photosynthesis. Write the equation.
Explain double circulation in humans with diagram.
Describe the structure and working of nephron with diagram.
Differentiate between arteries and veins.
tips: Digestive system mein enzymes yaad karo: Saliva→Amylase, Stomach→Pepsin, Pancreas→Trypsin, Lipase
Heart ka diagram MUST practice — left side mein oxygenated blood
Nephron diagram roz 1 baar draw karo — easy marks aayenge
Xylem = water (upward, dead cells), Phloem = food (both directions, living cells)

chapters.title: Control and Coordination
topics: Coordination in plants: Tropic and Nastic movements
Plant hormones: Auxin, Gibberellin, Cytokinin, Abscisic acid, Ethylene
importantQuestions: Draw a labelled diagram of a neuron.
What is a reflex action? Explain with a reflex arc diagram.
Name the parts of human brain and write one function of each.
How does chemical coordination occur in plants? Name different plant hormones.
What is the function of: (i) Insulin (ii) Thyroxine (iii) Adrenaline
tips: Neuron diagram draw karo: Dendrite → Cell body → Axon → Nerve ending
Brain parts: Cerebrum (thinking), Cerebellum (balance), Medulla (involuntary like breathing)
Plant hormones: Auxin (growth), Gibberellin (stem elongation), Cytokinin (cell division), ABA (inhibitor)
Endocrine glands mein pituitary = master gland kyunki yeh baaki glands ko control karta hai

chapters.title: How do Organisms Reproduce?
topics: Asexual reproduction: Fission, Fragmentation, Regeneration, Budding, Spore formation, Vegetative propagation
Human reproductive system — Male and Female
importantQuestions: Draw a diagram of: (i) Male reproductive system (ii) Female reproductive system
What is the significance of DNA copying in reproduction?
Describe the process of fertilization in flowers.
Differentiate between self-pollination and cross-pollination.
What are the different methods of contraception?
tips: Asexual methods: Amoeba→Binary fission, Hydra→Budding, Planaria→Regeneration, Rhizopus→Spore formation
Flower parts: Sepal, Petal, Stamen (anther+filament), Pistil (stigma+style+ovary)
Male system: Testis → Vas deferens → Urethra | Female: Ovary → Oviduct → Uterus
Menstrual cycle = 28 days, Ovulation = Day 14

chapters.title: Heredity and Evolution
importantQuestions: Explain Mendel's monohybrid cross with a cross diagram. State the ratios.
How is the sex of the child determined in human beings? Explain with diagram.
Differentiate between homologous and analogous organs with examples.
What are fossils? What do they tell us about evolution?
What is the difference between acquired and inherited traits?
tips: Monohybrid ratio = 3:1 (phenotype), 1:2:1 (genotype) — yeh ratta lagao
Dihybrid ratio = 9:3:3:1 — phenotypic ratio
Father determines sex: XY (father) × XX (mother) → XX (girl) or XY (boy)
Homologous = same origin, different function (arm, wing, flipper); Analogous = different origin, same function (wing of bird, wing of insect)

chapters.title: Light — Reflection and Refraction
topics: Reflection of light — Laws of reflection
Refraction of light — Laws of refraction (Snell's Law)
formulas: Snell's Law: n₁ sin i = n₂ sin r
Refractive index: n = sin i / sin r = c/v
Power of lens: P = 1/f (in metres), unit = Dioptre (D)
Concave mirror: f = negative, Convex mirror: f = positive
Concave lens: f = negative, Convex lens: f = positive
diagrams: Image formation by concave mirror (all positions)
Image formation by convex lens (all positions)
importantQuestions: An object is placed at 30cm from a concave mirror of focal length 15cm. Find image position and nature.
Draw ray diagrams for concave mirror when object is: (i) at C (ii) between F and C (iii) at F
A convex lens of focal length 20cm forms image at 60cm. Find object distance.
An object of 5cm height is placed at 20cm from a convex lens of focal length 15cm. Find image position, nature and size.
What is the power of a lens of focal length 25cm?
tips: Sign convention yaad karo (New Cartesian): Left of mirror/lens = negative, Right = positive
Mirror: 1/v + 1/u = 1/f | Lens: 1/v - 1/u = 1/f — signs alag hain, dhyaan se!
Concave mirror = converging, Convex mirror = diverging (and vice versa for lenses)
Ray diagram mein 3 special rays banao: parallel to axis, through centre, through focus
Power P = 100/f(cm) — shortcut jab f centimetres mein ho

chapters.title: Human Eye and Colourful World
topics: functioning of eye lens, retina, iris
Defects of vision: Myopia, Hypermetropia, Presbyopia
Atmospheric refraction — Twinkling of stars, Early sunrise/Late sunset
Scattering of light — Tyndall effect, Blue sky, Red at sunrise/sunset
formulas: Myopia: corrected by concave lens, f = -distance of far point
Angle of deviation: δ = i₁ + i₂ - A (prism)
importantQuestions: What is myopia? Draw a ray diagram to show how it is corrected.
What is dispersion of light? Draw diagram showing dispersion through a prism.
Why does the sky appear blue?
Why do stars twinkle?
Explain why the sun appears red at sunrise and sunset.
tips: Myopia = near objects clear, far objects blurry → Concave lens se correct
Hypermetropia = far objects clear, near objects blurry → Convex lens se correct
VIBGYOR: Violet, Indigo, Blue, Green, Yellow, Orange, Red — yaad rakho
Blue sky = Rayleigh scattering (blue light scattered most, λ chhoti), Red sunset = longer path, only red reaches

chapters.title: Electricity
topics: Series and Parallel combination of resistors
importantQuestions: State Ohm's law. Draw V-I characteristics graph.
Three resistors of 2Ω, 3Ω, 6Ω are connected in parallel. Find equivalent resistance.
An electric iron of 1000W is used for 30 minutes daily. Find cost of electricity for 30 days at ₹4 per unit.
Two resistors of 3Ω and 6Ω are connected in parallel, which is then connected in series with a 4Ω resistor. Find total resistance.
Why is parallel combination preferred in household wiring?
tips: Series mein current same, voltage divide | Parallel mein voltage same, current divide
Numericals mein units convert karo pehle — W, kW, Joule, kWh sab alag hain
Resistivity temperature-dependent hai but resistance bhi length aur area pe depend karta hai
Household mein parallel use hota hai — agar ek device band ho toh baaki chalu rehte hain
1 unit = 1 kWh = 1000W × 1 hour

chapters.title: Magnetic Effects of Electric Current
topics: Magnetic field due to current-carrying conductor
Magnetic field due to a solenoid
Force on a current-carrying conductor in magnetic field
formulas: Right-hand thumb rule: thumb = current direction, curled fingers = magnetic field direction
Fleming's Left-Hand Rule: Forefinger=Field, Middle finger=Current, Thumb=Force (Motor)
Fleming's Right-Hand Rule: Forefinger=Field, Thumb=Motion, Middle finger=Induced current (Generator)
diagrams: Magnetic field lines around straight conductor
importantQuestions: What is electromagnetic induction? Name the scientist associated with it.
Draw a labelled diagram of an electric motor. Explain its working.
Draw a labelled diagram of an AC generator. Explain its working.
State Fleming's Left-Hand Rule and Right-Hand Rule.
What is a solenoid? Draw magnetic field lines of a solenoid. Compare with a bar magnet.
tips: Motor = Electric energy → Mechanical energy (Left-Hand Rule) | Generator = Mechanical → Electric (Right-Hand Rule)
Motor mein split ring commutator, Generator mein slip rings — yeh difference important hai
Solenoid ka magnetic field bar magnet jaisa hota hai — diagram practice karo
Domestic supply: 220V, 50Hz AC | Fuse = safety device, always in LIVE wire
